scenarios.description: setScores updates scores and height when given a valid power-of-two array of length 8 (branch: valid length)
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance has initial scores of length 8; no stubs or mocks
scenarios.expectedResult: scores field is set to the provided array [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8]; height is set to 3
pseudocode: TEST TC01 setScores with valid power-of-two array

scenarios.description: setScores updates scores and height when given the minimum valid array of length 1 (branch: valid length boundary)
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance has initial scores of length 8; no stubs or mocks
scenarios.expectedResult: scores field is set to the provided array [42]; height is set to 0
pseudocode: TEST TC02 setScores with single-element array

scenarios.description: setScores rejects non–power-of-two length array and prints error (branch: invalid length)
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance initialized; System.out stubbed via ByteArrayOutputStream to capture output
scenarios.expectedResult: scores field remains unchanged; height remains as before; System.out prints "The number of scores must be a power of 2.\n"
pseudocode: TEST TC03 setScores with invalid non-power-of-two array
assert outContent.toString().equals("The number of scores must be a power of 2.\n");
